editor_settings.title: Is the Canon G7X Discontinued in 2026? The Official Answer
editor_settings.content:
Is the Canon G7X Discontinued in 2026? The Official Answer

As of May 3, 2026, the clean answer is no, not in the simple way people mean it.

Canon's own U.S. support pages are still live for the original G7 X, the G7 X Mark II, and the G7 X Mark III. Canon's U.S. store also still lists the Mark II and Mark III, even though one is out of stock and the other is sold out through Canon's queue system.

So if you are asking whether Canon has clearly buried the whole G7 X line, the answer is no.

The Strongest Official Clue

The biggest clue is not subtle.

Canon announced a PowerShot G7 X Mark III Special Edition on February 4, 2026 for the G series 30th anniversary. Companies do not roll out anniversary editions for a product line they have already mentally thrown in the trash.

That does not mean every version of the G7 X is easy to buy. It does mean the line is not officially dead in 2026.

Why the Rumor Keeps Spreading

The confusion makes sense if you are shopping instead of reading Canon press pages.

  • the Mark II shop page is listed as Out Of Stock
  • Canon's Mark III queue page currently shows the black model as sold out
  • used prices have been weird for a long time
  • retailers and resellers often look emptier than a healthy product line should

That is exactly why so many buyers jump from "I cannot find one" to "Canon must have discontinued it."

Those are not the same thing.

What This Means for Each Model

Original Canon G7 X
This one is old enough that nobody should confuse it with a fresh product. But Canon's support page is still up, which shows it has not vanished from Canon history.

Canon G7 X Mark II
This is where the rumor gets strongest. Canon still supports it and still lists it in the store, but the shopping reality feels rough enough that many people assume it is gone. It feels semi-ghosted even if Canon is not using the word discontinued.

Canon G7 X Mark III
This is the clearest proof the line still matters. Canon is still publicly queueing it, and the 2026 special-edition announcement makes it very hard to argue that the Mark III is officially finished.

So Is It Discontinued or Not?

If you want the simplest honest phrasing, use this:

The Canon G7 X line is not cleanly discontinued in Canon's official 2026 posture, but the buyer experience often feels like it is.

That sentence is uglier than a yes or no answer, but it is also more accurate.

What Buyers Should Do Next

If you just want a G7 X and do not want to fight the panic market, focus less on rumor wording and more on actual buy quality.

That means checking support status, current Canon pages, and whether a real listing makes more sense than waiting around for the perfect official restock fantasy.

If you want a clean G7 X Mark III without treating the sold-out queue like a death notice, this is the kind of listing that at least keeps the conversation practical:

[product-card:canon-powershot-g7-x-mark-iii-201mp-digital-camera-black-910-]


Final Verdict

No, the Canon G7 X is not plainly discontinued in 2026.

But yes, the way it is sold right now makes normal people think it is.

That is the whole problem. Canon's official signals say the line is still alive. The market signals say good luck finding one without confusion, queues, or hype pricing.
